attributes.stem: A 62-year-old man presents with a neck lump. The swelling is painless and has been present for 10 years. The size of the lump is constant, with no rapid progression in size, but the patient thinks it might have been getting bigger over the last year. There is no associated voice hoarseness, difficulty swallowing and no other constitutional symptoms. Physical examination reveals a compressible mass with a central comedone and no associated discharge (Image).
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
attributes.explanation:
There is a well-defined, smooth surfaced swelling, which appears to be very superficial. The most likely diagnosis is an epidermoid cyst. The length of the history virtually rules out all diagnoses out except for an epidermoid cyst or a lipoma. An epidermoid cyst is a benign cutaneous cyst which can appear anywhere in the body, but most commonly is found on the face, neck, chest, back and genitals.

Epidermoid cysts are usually painless and slow growing. However, if inflamed or infected, they can present as a painful erythematous swelling with occasional yellow discharge. Characteristic findings of epidermoid cyst includes a central punctum - as demonstrated in the picture above.

The most important condition to consider for a swelling in this site is a mixed parotid tumour (pleomorphic adenoma) in the lower aspect of the superficial lobe of the gland. However,in this case such a diagnosis is less likely due to the presence of central comedone which is more indicative of an epidermoid cyst. Patients with a pleomorphic adenoma of the parotid will most often present with a painless swelling anterior to the tragus, but an adenoma situated in the lower aspect of the superficial lobe can present with a swelling over the jaw. These tumours are usually more rapidly growing and a ten year history would be unusual.

A lipoma is unlikely as the swelling is too superficial and well-defined to be a lipoma. It is also unlikely for a lipoma to present with a central comedone.

Submandibular duct calculus presents with a painful swelling of the gland that is exacerbated prior or during eating.

Metastatic disease is unlikely due to the patient's history and examination findings. Palpable lymph nodes due to metastatic disease are non-compressible and progress more rapidly. Patients with metastatic disease are also more likely to present with constitutional symptoms of weight loss, fatigue and night sweats.
learningPoints: Characteristic findings of an epidermoid cyst include a painless, compressible lump with central punctum.
references: Trinh CT, Nguyen CH, Chansomphou V, Chansomphou V, Tran TT. Overview of epidermoid cyst. European journal of radiology open. 2019 Jan 1;6:291-301.
Jain S, Hasan S, Vyas N, Shah N, Dalal S. Pleomorphic adenoma of the parotid gland: report of a case with review of literature. Ethiopian journal of health sciences. 2015;25(2):189-94
Bokhari MR, Greene J. Pleomorphic Adenoma. StatPearls. Treasure Island (FL). 2020.
